Can you help me? Could you help me, please?
Could you send me the report? Can you lend me a pen?
Sure, of course. I am sorry, I cannot right now.
Could you check this email? Can you do me a favor?
Hi Rachel, can you help me for a minute?
Sure, Danilo. What do you need?
Could you send me the sales report, please? I cannot find it.
Of course. I can send it now. Anything else?
Yes. Could you also lend me your charger? My laptop is almost dead.
I am sorry, I cannot right now. I am using it. Maybe in ten minutes?
No problem. And can you check this email before I send it?
Hmm, I am a little busy now. Can you ask Marcus? He is available.
Good idea. Thank you for your help, Rachel.
You are welcome. Just do me a favor: send me the final file later.
Can you help me, please?
Could you send me the report, please?
Could you lend me your charger?
Can you do me a favor? Thank you for your help.

Hi Danilo, it's Rachel. I have a few favors to ask. First, could you send me the final sales report before noon? I need it for the client. Second, can you check the numbers on page two? I think there is a small mistake. And one more thing: I am very busy this afternoon, so could you call the client for me at three o'clock? If you can't, just let me know and I can ask Marcus. He is available today. Thank you so much for your help. Talk soon. Bye!

At work, I ask for help every day, and I always try to be polite. When I need something small, I say can you help me? When I want to be more polite, I say could you send me the file, please? If a colleague is busy, I never say do it now. I wait, or I ask another person. When someone helps me, I always say thank you. And when a colleague asks me for a favor, I say sure, of course if I am free, or I am sorry, I can't right now if I am busy. Polite words are small, but they make a big difference.
